differential_diagnoses: Medication-induced psychosis (due to antiseizure medications or benzodiazepine withdrawal)
Psychogenic nonepileptic seizures (PNES) with comorbid epilepsy
Delirium due to metabolic or systemic disturbance
next_steps: Continuous video-EEG monitoring to differentiate ictal/postictal states from interictal psychopathology
Comprehensive metabolic panel and toxicology screen to rule out acute precipitating factors
Review of all current medication dosages and recent titration schedules to assess for paradoxical drug effects
Neuropsychiatric consultation to refine diagnosis and manage behavioral agitation
Safety management including environmental precautions and judicious use of antipsychotics
In-depth collateral history gathering from family regarding pre-morbid personality and psychiatric history
Adjustment of antiseizure regimen toward agents with more favorable psychiatric side-effect profiles

differential_diagnoses: Psychogenic nonepileptic seizures (PNES) comorbid with epilepsy
next_steps: Perform lumbar puncture for CSF analysis, including autoimmune encephalitis panel (anti-LGI1, anti-NMDA, etc.)
Continue continuous video-EEG monitoring with expert review to correlate behavioral events with electrographic activity
Review current medication regimen and cautiously taper potential psychotropic-contributing medications while ensuring seizure protection
Conduct neuropsychiatric evaluation and thorough collateral history collection from family
Review and optimize seizure management to avoid excessive polypharmacy or rapid titration
Ensure strict patient safety measures including bedside precautions for agitation
